title: How to share a secret
quote: In this paper we show how to divide data D into n pieces in such a way that D is easily reconstructable from any k pieces, but even complete knowledge of k - 1 pieces reveals absolutely no information about D.

title: The Flexible Round-Optimized Schnorr Threshold (FROST) Protocol for Two-Round Schnorr Signatures
quote: FROST signatures can be issued after a threshold number of entities cooperate to compute a signature, allowing for improved distribution of trust and redundancy with respect to a secret key.

title: Fast Secure Two-Party ECDSA Signing
quote: We present a highly practical two-party signing protocol for ECDSA where two parties with secret-shares of a private key can jointly compute ECDSA signatures without revealing their secret shares to each other.

title: Secure Two-party Threshold ECDSA from ECDSA Assumptions
quote: Secure multi-party ECDSA requires custom protocols, which has heretofore implied reliance upon additional cryptographic assumptions and primitives such as the Paillier cryptosystem.

title: Threshold ECDSA from ECDSA Assumptions: The Multiparty Case
quote: We propose an extension of their scheme to arbitrary thresholds, and prove it secure against a malicious adversary corrupting up to one party less than the threshold under only the Computational Diffie-Hellman Assumption in the Global Random Oracle model.

title: Threshold ECDSA in Three Rounds
quote: We present a three-round protocol for threshold ECDSA signing with malicious security against a dishonest majority, which information-theoretically UC-realizes a standard threshold signing functionality, assuming only ideal commitment and two-party multiplication primitives.

title: Secure distributed key generation for discrete-log based cryptosystems
quote: A Distributed Key Generation (DKG) protocol is an essential component of threshold cryptosystems required to initialize the cryptosystem securely and generate its private and public keys.

title: Proactive Refresh for Accountable Threshold Signatures
quote: Proactive refresh is a protocol that lets the group of signers refresh their shares of the secret key, without changing the public key or the threshold.

title: Ethereum accounts
quote: Externally-owned account (EOA) – controlled by anyone with the private keys.

title: Smart Account Concepts
quote: Safe Smart Account maintains a threshold, the minimum number of owners required to confirm a transaction before it gets executed.

title: Transactions
quote: In multisig pubkey scripts, called m-of-n, m is the minimum number of signatures which must match a public key; n is the number of public keys being provided.

title: Multisignature vs MPC
quote: MPC is a cryptographic technique that allows multiple parties to jointly sign without revealing their private key material to each other.

title: Multi-party computation (MPC) Wallets
quote: Unlike multisignature wallets, which require multiple signatures, MPC wallets only need a single signature for transactions.

title: Introduction to MPC
quote: To sign a transaction, a minimum number (a threshold) of these shares are required to participate in a cryptographic ceremony. The key itself is never reconstructed during this process.

title: Embedded Wallet FAQ
quote: A self-custodial crypto wallet built into your app. Users sign in with email OTP instead of managing seed phrases while maintaining full control of their assets.

title: Multi-party Computation (MPC) wallet infrastructure for wallets and dApps
quote: Web3Auth MPC is blockchain-agnostic. Use it on any chain or protocol like ECDSA, EDDSA, BLS Support among others.

title: silence-laboratories/dkls23
quote: DKLs23 is a high-performance threshold ECDSA signing protocol with dynamic quorum management.

title: BitGo Wallet Types
quote: Most UTXO-based blockchains, such as Bitcoin, natively support multisignature wallets. Account-based blockchains, such as Ethereum, all support MPC.

title: Threshold ECDSA in Three Rounds
quote: Our work improves upon and fully subsumes the DKLs t-of-n and 2-of-n protocols.

title: silent-shard-dkls23-ll
quote: These libraries are low-level. They provide foundational building blocks for real-world applications but intentionally exclude many crucial components typically required in secure applications.

title: Overview - Safe Smart Account
quote: Smart accounts do not rely on a single private key. Instead, on-chain smart contract logic defines who can execute transactions, under which conditions, and how they are validated.
